title: The Problem of Noise Pollution
description: For questions 17 – 24, read the text below. Use the word given in capitals at the end of some of the lines to form a word that fits in the gap in the same line. There is an example at the beginning (0).

content.text: Noise (0) pollution is increasingly recognised as a serious public health issue in many countries. (17) Excessive levels of noise from traffic, construction, and aircraft can cause stress, sleep (18) disturbance, and even long-term hearing damage. Studies have shown that people living in noisy areas tend to be less (19) productive and more prone to anxiety.||The (20) psychological effects of constant noise should not be underestimated. Urban planners are now designing (21) quieter streets by incorporating noise barriers and green spaces. (22) Legislation requiring businesses and local authorities to reduce noise levels is also becoming more common. Raising public (23) awareness of the issue is essential so that individuals can take (24) preventive steps to protect their own hearing and wellbeing.

example.text: Noise (0) POLLUTION is increasingly recognised as a serious public health issue in many countries. (POLLUTE)

questions.explanation: The adjective 'excessive' is formed from 'excess' by adding the suffix '-ive'.
The noun 'disturbance' is formed from 'disturb' by adding the suffix '-ance'.
The adjective 'productive' is formed from 'produce' via the root 'product' + '-ive'.
The adjective 'psychological' is formed from 'psyche' via 'psychology' + '-ical'.
The comparative adjective 'quieter' is formed from 'quiet' by adding the suffix '-er'.
The noun 'legislation' is formed from 'legislate' by replacing '-ate' with '-ation'.
The noun 'awareness' is formed from 'aware' by adding the suffix '-ness'.
The adjective 'preventive' is formed from 'prevent' by adding the suffix '-ive'.
